Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given an event log and reference tables We need to identify anomalies and undesirable behaviors hypothesize reasons and propose SQL queries to investigate

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First lets list the expected normal flow 1 Register Order Sales 2 Perform Credit Check Finance 3 Validate Stock Warehouse 4 Confirm Shipment Logistics 5 Ship Goods Logistics 6 Issue Invoice Finance 7 Receive Payment Finance

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Events in order 1 Register Order SalesRep 01 2 Perform Credit Check CreditOfficer 1 3 Validate Stock WarehouseClerk 2 4 Confirm Shipment LogisticsMgr 1 5 Ship Goods WarehouseClerk 3 6 Issue Invoice FinanceTeam 01 7 Receive Payment FinanceTeam 02 This follows the normal flow exactly

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Events 1 Register Order SalesRep 02 2 Confirm Shipment LogisticsMgr 2 This is out of order Expected after Validate Stock but it occurs before Credit Check and Validate Stock 3 Ship Goods WarehouseClerk 1 Even more out of order Expected after Confirm Shipment but here it is before Credit Check and Validate Stock 4 Perform Credit Check CreditOfficer 2 5 Validate Stock WarehouseClerk 2 6 Issue Invoice FinanceTeam 03 7 Receive Payment FinanceTeam 02

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So in case 1002 we see that Confirm Shipment and Ship Goods are performed before the Credit Check and Stock Validation This is an anomaly

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Events 1 Register Order SalesRep 03 2 Perform Credit Check CreditOfficer 1 3 Ship Goods WarehouseClerk 3 Out of order Expected after Validate Stock and Confirm Shipment but here it is before Validate Stock and Confirm Shipment 4 Issue Invoice FinanceTeam 01 This is also out of order Expected after Ship Goods and before Receive Payment but here it is before Confirm Shipment and after Ship Goods which is also out of order 5 Confirm Shipment LogisticsMgr 2 This is late and after Ship Goods and Issue Invoice 6 Receive Payment FinanceTeam 02

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So in case 1003 we see that Ship Goods and Issue Invoice are performed before Confirm Shipment Also note that Validate Stock is missing entirely

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Events 1 Register Order SalesRep 01 2 Receive Payment FinanceTeam 02 This is very early before any other steps 3 Issue Invoice FinanceTeam 01 Invoice issued after payment Unusual 4 Confirm Shipment LogisticsMgr 1 After payment and invoice but note that shipment scheduled N in additional info 5 Ship Goods WarehouseClerk 4

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In case 1004 we see that payment is received before the credit check stock validation and even before the invoice is issued Also the credit check and stock validation are missing The Confirm Shipment has shipment scheduled N which might indicate a problem

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Case 1002 and 1003 show out of order activities This could be due to human error or lack of training resources performing tasks without following the process system issues maybe the system allows tasks to be performed in any order policy violations maybe some orders like priority orders are allowed to be processed faster skipping some checks But note that 1002 is a priority order but 1003 is standard

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Missing activities in Case 1003 and 1004 In 1003 Validate Stock is missing This could be a data entry error not logged or actually skipped In 1004 both Credit Check and Validate Stock are missing This might be because the payment was received early maybe its a prepaid order from a trusted customer

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Early payment in Case 1004 This could be due to the order being paid in advance or a system error in logging the events maybe the payment was recorded at the wrong time

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Invoice after payment in Case 1004 This is unusual because typically an invoice is issued and then payment is received This could be a data entry error or a different payment method like cash on delivery where the payment is received and then the invoice is issued for record keeping

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Confirm Shipment after Ship Goods in Case 1003 This is a violation of the process It might be that the confirmation was done late or back dated

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - IND
Text: In Case 1002 Confirm Shipment and Ship Goods are done before Credit Check and Stock Validation This is a risk because if the credit check fails or stock is not available then the shipment would have been scheduled and even shipped unnecessarily

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In Case 1004 the Confirm Shipment has shipment scheduled N This might indicate that the shipment was not scheduled but then Ship Goods event exists This could be a data inconsistency or a problem in the process maybe the shipment was not scheduled but still shipped

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets propose SQL queries to investigate these anomalies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 1 Check for missing activities in cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 2 Check the order of activities for each case and compare with the expected order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 3 Check for cases where payment is received before the invoice is issued

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 4 Check for cases where shipment is confirmed after goods are shipped

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 5 Check for cases where credit check is performed after shipment activities

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 6 Check for cases where stock validation is missing and the order was shipped

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 7 Check for cases with early payment payment before all other activities except order registration

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Query 8 Check for orders that are marked as priority and see if they have different patterns We can join with the orders table to see if order type affects the process

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Query 9 Check for resources that are performing activities out of order We can check for resources that are performing activities in the wrong order by comparing with the normal flow and see if there are patterns by role or department
